name: Navbar Menu
summary: A hover-activated navigation menu with spring-animated dropdown panels and layout transition effects.
detailedDescription: Navbar Menu creates an interactive navigation bar with hover-activated dropdown panels using Framer Motion. The Menu component provides a rounded-full container with shadow-input, white/dark background, and border styling. MenuItem components trigger dropdowns on mouseEnter, with layoutId='active' enabling smooth shared element transitions between menu items. The dropdown panel uses spring physics (mass: 0.5, damping: 11.5, stiffness: 100) for enter animations - starting at opacity 0, scale 0.85, y: 10 and animating to full visibility. Position is absolute at top + 1.2rem, centered with -translate-x-1/2. The panel features rounded-2xl corners, backdrop-blur-sm, and shadow-xl for depth. ProductItem subcomponent displays product cards with 140x70 images, title, and description in horizontal layout. HoveredLink provides styled link transitions. The nav resets state on mouseLeave. This creates polished mega-menu navigation ideal for product showcases, documentation sites, or e-commerce where rich hover previews enhance navigation.
purpose: Display rich product previews in menus
Animate menu transitions with spring physics
behavior: Uses layoutId for shared element transitions
recommendations: Good fit for dashboards, admin panels, and data-dense application layouts.
Suitable for performance-sensitive pages that still need polished visuals.
Combine with hero and feature sections to establish clear top-level page flow.
